Why are you interested in this role?: What pulls me to this role is the combination of enablement and change management—two things I genuinely love. Most enablement fails not because the content is bad but because the change doesn't stick. At Vestas I led an Inclusive Leadership initiative using ADKAR: I built awareness, found champions, developed curriculum with an SME, and ran reinforcement until it scaled to 23 locations. An Enablement Business Partner role for Customer Success is exactly that work—diagnosing where a team underperforms, building the curriculum to close the gap, and managing the change so it holds.
Why do you want to work at Samsara?: Two reasons. First, the work is the work I do best—I run 20+ enablement programs today, deliver training to 600+ people, and build curriculum across formats. Second, Samsara is scaling fast and globally, and change-heavy, high-growth environments are where enablement matters most and where I'm most energized. I'll be honest that I haven't carried a post-sale CSM or AM title, so I'd be learning the specifics of the CS motion. But the enablement and change-management core transfers directly, and I ramp quickly on new domains and products.
How do you design an enablement program: I start with a needs assessment, not content. I run 1:1s and look at performance data to find the REAL gap rather than the assumed one, then define success in measurable behavioral terms—not activity. I partner with SMEs who own the content while I own the pedagogy, deliver it in layers (live plus async plus follow-up), and measure whether behavior actually changed, iterating with stakeholders. That's how I cut onboarding time 75% at InGenius. The discipline I hold to is: assumption is the most expensive thing in enablement, so diagnose before you design.
Tell us about yourself / anything else: I'm an analytical organizer with a growth mindset, grounded in an MS in Applied Organizational Psychology and a BA in Psychology and Education Studies—so learning and change are genuinely my training. I diagnose with data, collaborate cross-functionally on the intervention, and track behavioral signals to make sure it sticks. I'm familiar with ADKAR and the Kübler-Ross change curve and would love to formalize that with Prosci certification. I'm honest about the post-sale experience I'm still building, and confident in the enablement and change-management strengths I'd bring on day one.
